description_long: This object consists of two long swords, each featuring a straight, double-edged blade with a pointed tip, ideal for thrusting and slashing. The blades are metallic and have a dark, slightly reflective finish, suggesting they are made of steel or a similar alloy. Both swords have prominent crossguards, providing hand protection, and the handles are cylindrical, wrapped in a dark material, possibly leather, for improved grip. The pommels are rounded and simple, with one sword showing a ring at the end, possibly for attaching a lanyard or strap. The overall design is functional and robust, with minimal ornamentation, suitable for practical use rather than ceremonial display. The swords are accompanied by matching sheaths, which are dark and appear to be made of leather or a similar material, fitted to the blades for secure carrying.
description: A pair of long, straight, double-edged swords with dark metallic blades, crossguards, and wrapped handles, each accompanied by a matching sheath.
description_view_0: Front view showing both swords sheathed, highlighting the cylindrical handles and crossguards.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the blades and sheaths, emphasizing the straight, pointed geometry.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the handles and pommels, with the crossguards clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the alignment of the blades and sheaths, with the handles and crossguards in profile.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, wrapped in dark material, simple rounded pommel with ring on one handle
